name: South Azerbaijani

ancestors.notes: Persian adstrate: lexicon and the Perso-Arabic writing tradition.

notes: Grapheme inventory NOT YET SPECIFIED (stub). South Azerbaijani (Iran) is written in the Perso-Arabic script — an abjad in which short vowels are largely unwritten, although the Iranian Azerbaijani norm uses matres lectionis (ا آ و ی) and optional diacritics more heavily than Persian does. The Latin alphabet of North Azerbaijani (see `az`) is a different orthography and is not modelled here. No Arabic letter-to-phoneme map is claimed: a Tier-A spec would have to restore unwritten short vowels and Azerbaijani vowel harmony, which needs vocalised input or a lexicon.
